He Was Not Willing

Words: Lucy R. Meyer, 1885. Music and · Music: Lucy R. Meyer, 1885

Phrase A · bars 1–8melody rises — closes home3rds CCW
G4 Shading1
233 B33
IV
ii

133 933
ii
I
B♭4 Exploring8
133 B33
IV
I
v.1"He was not willing that any should perish"; Jesus enthroned in the glory above, Saw our poor fallen world, pitied our sorrows, Poured out His life for us, wonderful love! Perishing, perishing! Thronging our pathway, Hearts break with burdens too heavy to bear: Jesus would save, but there's no one to tell them, No one to lift them from sin and despair.
v.2"He was not willing that any should perish"; Clothed in our flesh with its sorrow and pain, Came He to seek the lost, comfort the mourner, Heal the heart broken by sorrow and shame. Perishing, perishing! Harvest is passing, Reapers are few and the night draweth near: Jesus is calling thee, haste to the reaping, Thou shalt have souls, precious souls for thy hire.
